NKJV.1: Therefore I exhort first of all that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks be made for all men,
NKJV.2: for kings and all who are in authority, that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and reverence.
NKJV.3: For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior,
NKJV.4: who desires all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
NKJV.5: For there is one God and one Mediator between God and men, the Man Christ Jesus,
NKJV.6: who gave Himself a ransom for all, to be testified in due time,
NKJV.7: for which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle—I am speaking the truth in Christ and not lying— a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
NKJV.8: I desire therefore that the men pray everywhere, lifting up holy hands, without wrath and doubting;
NKJV.9: in like manner also, that the women adorn themselves in modest apparel, with propriety and moderation, not with braided hair or gold or pearls or costly clothing,
NKJV.10: but, which is proper for women professing godliness, with good works.
NKJV.11: Let a woman learn in silence with all submission.
NKJV.12: And I do not permit a woman to teach or to have authority over a man, but to be in silence.
NKJV.13: For Adam was formed first, then Eve.
NKJV.14: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman being deceived, fell into transgression.
NKJV.15: Nevertheless she will be saved in childbearing if they continue in faith, love, and holiness, with self-control.

ESV.1: First of all, then, I urge that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for all people,
ESV.2: for kings and all who are in high positions, that we may lead a peaceful and quiet life, godly and dignified in every way.
ESV.3: This is good, and it is pleasing in the sight of God our Savior,
ESV.4: who desires all people to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
ESV.5: For there is one God, and there is one mediator between God and men, the man  Christ Jesus,
ESV.6: who gave himself as a ransom for all, which is the testimony given at the proper time.
ESV.7: For this I was appointed a preacher and an apostle (I am telling the truth, I am not lying), a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
ESV.8: I desire then that in every place the men should pray, lifting holy hands without anger or quarreling;
ESV.9: likewise also that women should adorn themselves in respectable apparel, with modesty and self-control, not with braided hair and gold or pearls or costly attire,
ESV.10: but with what is proper for women who profess godliness — with good works.
ESV.11: Let a woman learn quietly with all submissiveness.
ESV.12: I do not permit a woman to teach or to exercise authority over a man; rather, she is to remain quiet.
ESV.13: For Adam was formed first, then Eve;
ESV.14: and Adam was not deceived, but the woman was deceived and became a transgressor.
ESV.15: Yet she will be saved through childbearing — if they continue in faith and love and holiness, with self-control.

NASB.1: First of all, then, I urge that entreaties [and] prayers, petitions [and] thanksgivings, be made on behalf of all men,
NASB.2: for kings and all who are in authority, so that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness and dignity.
NASB.3: This is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior,
NASB.4: who desires all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
NASB.5: For there is one God, [and] one mediator also between God and men, [the] man Christ Jesus,
NASB.6: who gave Himself as a ransom for all, the testimony [given] at the proper time.
NASB.7: For this I was appointed a preacher and an apostle (I am telling the truth, I am not lying) as a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
NASB.8: Therefore I want the men in every place to pray, lifting up holy hands, without wrath and dissension.
NASB.9: Likewise, [I want] women to adorn themselves with proper clothing, modestly and discreetly, not with braided hair and gold or pearls or costly garments,
NASB.10: but rather by means of good works, as is proper for women making a claim to godliness.
NASB.11: A woman must quietly receive instruction with entire submissiveness.
NASB.12: But I do not allow a woman to teach or exercise authority over a man, but to remain quiet.
NASB.13: For it was Adam who was first created, [and] then Eve.
NASB.14: And [it was] not Adam [who] was deceived, but the woman being deceived, fell into transgression.
NASB.15: But [women] will be preserved through the bearing of children if they continue in faith and love and sanctity with self-restraint.

NLT.1: I urge you, first of all, to pray for all people. Ask God to help them; intercede on their behalf, and give thanks for them.
NLT.2: Pray this way for kings and all who are in authority so that we can live peaceful and quiet lives marked by godliness and dignity.
NLT.3: This is good and pleases God our Savior,
NLT.4: who wants everyone to be saved and to understand the truth.
NLT.5: For, There is one God and one Mediator who can reconcile God and humanity — the man Christ Jesus.
NLT.6:
He gave his life to purchase freedom for everyone.
This is the message God gave to the world at just the right time.
NLT.7: And I have been chosen as a preacher and apostle to teach the Gentiles this message about faith and truth. I’m not exaggerating — just telling the truth.
NLT.8: In every place of worship, I want men to pray with holy hands lifted up to God, free from anger and controversy.
NLT.9: And I want women to be modest in their appearance. They should wear decent and appropriate clothing and not draw attention to themselves by the way they fix their hair or by wearing gold or pearls or expensive clothes.
NLT.10: For women who claim to be devoted to God should make themselves attractive by the good things they do.
NLT.11: Women should learn quietly and submissively.
NLT.12: I do not let women teach men or have authority over them. Let them listen quietly.
NLT.13: For God made Adam first, and afterward he made Eve.
NLT.14: And it was not Adam who was deceived by Satan. The woman was deceived, and sin was the result.
NLT.15: But women will be saved through childbearing, assuming they continue to live in faith, love, holiness, and modesty.

WEB.1: I exhort therefore, first of all, that petitions, prayers, intercessions, and givings of thanks, be made for all men:
WEB.2: for kings and all who are in high places; that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness and reverence.
WEB.3: For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior;
WEB.4: who desires all people to be saved and come to full knowledge of the truth.
WEB.5: For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus,
WEB.6: who gave himself as a ransom for all; the testimony in its own times;
WEB.7: to which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle (I am telling the truth in Christ, not lying), a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
WEB.8: I desire therefore that the men in every place pray, lifting up holy hands without anger and doubting.
WEB.9: In the same way, that women also adorn themselves in decent clothing, with modesty and propriety; not just  with braided hair, gold, pearls, or expensive clothing;
WEB.10: but (which becomes women professing godliness) with good works.
WEB.11: Let a woman learn in quietness with full submission.
WEB.12: But I don’t permit a woman to teach, nor to exercise authority over a man, but to be in quietness.
WEB.13: For Adam was first formed, then Eve.
WEB.14: Adam wasn’t deceived, but the woman, being deceived, has fallen into disobedience;
WEB.15: but she will be saved through her childbearing, if they continue in faith, love, and sanctification with sobriety.

CSB17.1: First of all, then, I urge that petitions, prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for everyone,
CSB17.2: for kings and all those who are in authority, <sup>ⓑ</sup> so that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness <sup>ⓒ</sup> and dignity. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.3: This is good, and it pleases God our Savior, <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.4: who wants everyone <sup>ⓕ</sup> to be saved <sup>ⓖ</sup> and to come to the knowledge of the truth. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.5: For there is one God <sup>ⓘ</sup> and one mediator <sup>ⓙ</sup> between God and humanity, the man <sup>ⓚ</sup> Christ Jesus,
CSB17.6: who gave himself as a ransom for all, <sup>ⓛ</sup> a testimony at the proper time.
CSB17.7: For this I was appointed a herald, an apostle <sup>ⓜ</sup> (I am telling the truth; <sup>[1]</sup> I am not lying), and a teacher of the Gentiles <sup>ⓝ</sup> in faith and truth.
CSB17.8: Therefore, I want the men in every place to pray, lifting up holy hands without anger or argument. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.9: Also, the women are to dress themselves in modest clothing, <sup>ⓑ</sup> with decency and good sense, not with elaborate hairstyles, gold, <sup>ⓒ</sup> pearls, or expensive apparel,
CSB17.10: but with good works, <sup>ⓓ</sup> as is proper for women who profess to worship God.
CSB17.11: A woman is to learn quietly with full submission. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.12: I do not allow a woman to teach or to have authority over a man; instead, she is to remain quiet.
CSB17.13: For Adam was formed first, then Eve. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.14: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman was deceived and transgressed. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.15: But she will be saved through childbearing, if they continue in faith, love, and holiness, <sup>ⓗ</sup> with good sense.

AMP.1: First of all, then, I urge that petitions (specific requests), prayers, intercessions (prayers for others) and thanksgivings be offered on behalf of all people,
AMP.2: for kings and all who are in [positions of] high authority, so that we may live a peaceful and quiet life in all godliness and dignity.
AMP.3: This [kind of praying] is good and acceptable and pleasing in the sight of God our Savior,
AMP.4: who wishes all people to be saved and to come to the knowledge and recognition of the [divine] truth.
AMP.5: For there is [only] one God, and [only] one Mediator between God and mankind, the Man Christ Jesus,
AMP.6: who gave Himself as a ransom [a substitutionary sacrifice to atone] for all, the testimony given at the right and proper time.
AMP.7: And for this matter I was appointed a preacher and an apostle—I am telling the truth, I am not lying [when I say this]—a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
AMP.8: Therefore I want the men in every place to pray, lifting up holy hands, without anger and disputing or quarreling or doubt [in their mind].
AMP.9: Likewise, I want women to adorn themselves modestly and appropriately and discreetly in proper clothing, not with [elaborately] braided hair and gold or pearls or expensive clothes,
AMP.10: but instead adorned by good deeds [helping others], as is proper for women who profess to worship God.
AMP.11: A woman must quietly receive instruction with all submissiveness.
AMP.12: I do not allow a woman to teach or exercise authority over a man, but to remain quiet [in the congregation].
AMP.13: For Adam was formed first [by God from the earth], then Eve;
AMP.14: and it was not Adam who was deceived, but the woman who was led astray and fell into sin.
AMP.15: But women will be preserved (saved) through [the pain and dangers of] the bearing of children if they continue in faith and love and holiness with self-control and discretion.

MSG.1: The first thing I want you to do is pray. Pray every way you know how, for everyone you know.
MSG.2: Pray especially for rulers and their governments to rule well so we can be quietly about our business of living simply, in humble contemplation.
MSG.3: This is the way our Savior God wants us to live.
MSG.4: He wants not only us but everyone saved, you know, everyone to get to know the truth we've learned:
MSG.5: that there's one God and only one, and one Priest-Mediator between God and us—Jesus,
MSG.6: who offered himself in exchange for everyone held captive by sin, to set them all free. Eventually the news is going to get out.
MSG.7: This and this only has been my appointed work: getting this news to those who have never heard of God, and explaining how it works by simple faith and plain truth.
MSG.8: Since prayer is at the bottom of all this, what I want mostly is for men to pray—not shaking angry fists at enemies but raising holy hands to God.
MSG.9: And I want women to get in there with the men in humility before God, not primping before a mirror or chasing the latest fashions
MSG.10: but doing something beautiful for God and becoming beautiful doing it.
MSG.11: I don't let women take over and tell the men what to do.
MSG.12: They should study to be quiet and obedient along with everyone else.
MSG.13: Adam was made first, then Eve;
MSG.14: woman was deceived first—our pioneer in sin!—with Adam right on her heels.
MSG.15: On the other hand, her childbearing brought about salvation, reversing Eve. But this salvation only comes to those who continue in faith, love, and holiness, gathering it all into maturity. You can depend on this.

NIV.1:
Instructions on Worship
I urge, then, first of all, that requests, prayers, intercession and thanksgiving be made for everyone—
NIV.2: for kings and all those in authority, that we may live peaceful and quiet lives in all godliness and holiness.
NIV.3: This is good, and pleases God our Savior,
NIV.4: who wants all men to be saved and to come to a knowledge of the truth.
NIV.5: For there is one God and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus,
NIV.6: who gave himself as a ransom for all men—the testimony given in its proper time.
NIV.7: And for this purpose I was appointed a herald and an apostle—I am telling the truth, I am not lying—and a teacher of the true faith to the Gentiles.
NIV.8: I want men everywhere to lift up holy hands in prayer, without anger or disputing.
NIV.9: I also want women to dress modestly, with decency and propriety, not with braided hair or gold or pearls or expensive clothes,
NIV.10: but with good deeds, appropriate for women who profess to worship God.
NIV.11: A woman should learn in quietness and full submission.
NIV.12: I do not permit a woman to teach or to have authority over a man; she must be silent.
NIV.13: For Adam was formed first, then Eve.
NIV.14: And Adam was not the one deceived; it was the woman who was deceived and became a sinner.
NIV.15: But women will be saved through childbearing—if they continue in faith, love and holiness with propriety.

NRSVCE.1: First of all, then, I urge that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for everyone,
NRSVCE.2: for kings and all who are in high positions, so that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and dignity.
NRSVCE.3: This is right and is acceptable in the sight of God our Savior,
NRSVCE.4: who desires everyone to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
NRSVCE.5:
For there is one God;
there is also one mediator between God and humankind,
Christ Jesus, himself human,
NRSVCE.6:
who gave himself a ransom for all
— this was attested at the right time.
NRSVCE.7: For this I was appointed a herald and an apostle (I am telling the truth, I am not lying), a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
NRSVCE.8: I desire, then, that in every place the men should pray, lifting up holy hands without anger or argument;
NRSVCE.9: also that the women should dress themselves modestly and decently in suitable clothing, not with their hair braided, or with gold, pearls, or expensive clothes,
NRSVCE.10: but with good works, as is proper for women who profess reverence for God.
NRSVCE.11: Let a woman learn in silence with full submission.
NRSVCE.12: I permit no woman to teach or to have authority over a man; she is to keep silent.
NRSVCE.13: For Adam was formed first, then Eve;
NRSVCE.14: and Adam was not deceived, but the woman was deceived and became a transgressor.
NRSVCE.15: Yet she will be saved through childbearing, provided they continue in faith and love and holiness, with modesty.

NET.1: First of all, then, I urge that requests, prayers, intercessions, and thanks be offered on behalf of all people,
NET.2: even for kings and all who are in authority, that we may lead a peaceful and quiet life in all godliness and dignity.
NET.3: Such prayer for all is good and welcomed before God our Savior,
NET.4: since he wants all people to be saved and to come to a knowledge of the truth.
NET.5: For there is one God and one intermediary between God and humanity, Christ Jesus, himself human,
NET.6: who gave himself as a ransom for all, revealing God’s purpose at his appointed time.
NET.7: For this I was appointed a preacher and apostle — I am telling the truth; I am not lying — and a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
NET.8: So I want the men to pray in every place, lifting up holy hands without anger or dispute.
NET.9: Likewise the women are to dress in suitable apparel, with modesty and self-control. Their adornment must not be with braided hair and gold or pearls or expensive clothing,
NET.10: but with good deeds, as is proper for women who profess reverence for God.
NET.11: A woman must learn quietly with all submissiveness.
NET.12: But I do not allow a woman to teach or exercise authority over a man. She must remain quiet.
NET.13: For Adam was formed first and then Eve.
NET.14: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman, because she was fully deceived, fell into transgression.
NET.15: But she will be delivered through childbearing, if she continues in faith and love and holiness with self-control.
